text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
55-year-old female with known metastatic melanoma, BRAF V600E mutant, Stage IV M1c. Prior treatment with dabra/Mekinist (approximately 8 months), discontinued at disease progression. Subsequently initiated on ipi 3 mg/kg plus nivo 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks; completed 3 of 4 planned combination cycles before treatment was held due to Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis requiring hospitalization and infliximab. Currently on oral prednisone taper. This study obtained for interval restaging approximately 14 weeks after initiation of combination immunotherapy. Comparison: Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis dated at time of progression on BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis obtained following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast. Images acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal reformats reviewed. Oral contrast not administered per departmental protocol.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidation. No ground-glass opacities to suggest immune-related pneumonitis. The airways are patent. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No thymic mass.

Axillary Regions: Previously enlarged right axillary lymph node has decreased in size, now measuring 1.6 cm in short axis (previously 2.8 cm at time of progression). Left axillary region: no pathologically enlarged nodes.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: No subcutaneous nodules are identified. Previously visualized cutaneous/subcutaneous soft tissue deposits on the trunk are no longer apparent.

Bones (chest): No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesions.

NECK BASES / CERVICAL REGION (included on scout and uppermost chest slices):
Right level II cervical region: a lymph node measuring approximately 1.4 cm is present (previously 2.2 cm at progression). Slight residual low attenuation within the node is noted but the central necrotic component seen on prior study appears less prominent.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour. There are several focal hypoattenuating lesions: the dominant segment 6 lesion now measures approximately 1.0 cm (previously 1.4 cm). A second lesion in the right lobe that previously measured 1.5 cm now measures approximately 0.9 cm. A third lesion previously measuring 0.8 cm appears stable at 0.8 cm. A fourth previously identified small right lobe lesion is no longer convincingly identified on this study. No new hepatic lesions. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. No ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands: No adrenal masses.

Kidneys: Normal in size and enhancement bilaterally. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. Aorta normal.

PERITONEUM:
Previously identified small peritoneal nodule adjacent to the falciform ligament: this lesion appears stable, measuring approximately 0.9 cm on the current study (unchanged from prior). No ascites. No new peritoneal implants identified.

PELVIS:

Bilateral inguinal regions: No pathologically enlarged inguinal lymph nodes. Previously documented bilateral inguinal adenopathy (which had resolved on BRAF/MEK therapy and subsequently was not noted to have recurred) remains absent.

Pelvic organs: Uterus and bilateral adnexa appear unremarkable. No pelvic free fluid. Urinary bladder unremarkable.

Bones (abdomen/pelvis): No focal suspicious osseous lesions.

BOWEL:
Small bowel appears normal in caliber and wall thickness. There is mild diffuse circumferential wall thickening of the sigmoid colon and rectum, up to approximately 5-6 mm, associated with mild pericolonic stranding. This finding is considerably improved compared to what would be expected in the acute setting and is most consistent with resolving immune-related colitis given the provided clinical history. No bowel obstruction. No pneumoperitoneum.

OTHER:
No suspicious soft tissue masses. No ascites. Visualized osseous structures demonstrate no aggressive lesion. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine noted, age-appropriate.

LABORATORY CORRELATION (provided by ordering clinician):
LDH: 241 U/L (elevated above institutional reference range of <225 U/L, down from 389 U/L at progression).

IMPRESSION:

1. Overall favorable response to immunotherapy across the majority of disease sites compared to the prior study at time of BRAF/MEK progression. Right axillary lymph node has decreased from 2.8 cm to 1.6 cm. Right cervical lymph node (level II) has decreased from 2.2 cm to 1.4 cm with less prominent necrotic change. Dominant hepatic segment 6 lesion decreased from 1.4 cm to 1.0 cm; a second right lobe hepatic lesion decreased from 1.5 cm to 0.9 cm; one smaller hepatic lesion no longer visualized.

2. Peritoneal nodule adjacent to the falciform ligament remains stable at 0.9 cm — no regression at this time point.

3. No new sites of disease. No intrathoracic nodal disease, no new pulmonary lesions, no new peritoneal, osseous, or soft tissue deposits.

4. No imaging findings to suggest immune-related pneumonitis.

5. Mild sigmoid and rectal wall thickening consistent with resolving immune-mediated colitis, improved in the clinical context of ongoing steroid taper after infliximab treatment.

6. LDH trending down at 241 U/L (from 389 U/L at progression), consistent with improving disease burden.

In aggregate, the pattern of nodal and hepatic regression with a stable peritoneal focus represents a meaningful response to the combination immunotherapy regimen. No evidence of disease progression.
